additionalTime: 10 minutes quick soak plus 10 minutes coating rest
author: Robert Carr
description: A quick hot honey chicken cutlet sandwich with thin chicken breasts, a buttermilk-hot sauce dip, crisp panko crust, garlic mayo, lime slaw, dill pickles, and toasted potato buns.
equipment: Cast-iron skillet or heavy saute pan

groceryIngredients.item: plain shredded cabbage and carrot coleslaw mix
groceryIngredients.display: plain shredded cabbage and carrot coleslaw mix

ingredients: 1 1/2 pounds boneless skinless chicken breasts, usually 2 large breasts, split horizontally into 4 cutlets
2 1/2 teaspoons Diamond Crystal kosher salt or 1 1/2 teaspoons Morton kosher salt, divided
1 1/4 teaspoons black pepper, freshly ground if possible, divided
2 tablespoons Louisiana-style cayenne hot sauce, such as Frank's RedHot, Crystal, or Texas Pete, divided
1 tablespoon plus 1 teaspoon Dijon mustard, divided
1 (16-ounce) jar dill pickle chips, using 1 cup pickle chips and 3 tablespoons pickle brine
1 1/2 cups avocado oil or another neutral high-heat oil, for shallow frying
3 cups plain shredded cabbage and carrot coleslaw mix, from a 10-ounce bag
1 lime, juiced to yield 1 tablespoon lime juice
1 tablespoon plus 1 teaspoon apple cider vinegar, divided
instructions: Set 1 wire rack inside a rimmed baking sheet for the breaded raw chicken, then set a second clean wire rack inside another rimmed baking sheet for the fried chicken.
Split the 1 1/2 pounds boneless skinless chicken breasts horizontally into 4 cutlets if they are not already cut thin.
Place the cutlets between sheets of plastic wrap or parchment and pound them to an even 1/2 inch thickness.
Season both sides of the 4 chicken cutlets with 1 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt and 1/2 teaspoon black pepper total.
In a shallow bowl, whisk together 3/4 cup buttermilk, 1 large egg, 1 tablespoon Louisiana-style cayenne hot sauce, 1 tablespoon Dijon mustard, and 1 tablespoon pickle brine from the dill pickle jar.
Add the seasoned chicken cutlets to the buttermilk mixture and turn each piece until fully coated.
Let the chicken sit in the buttermilk mixture at room temperature for 10 minutes while making the sauce and slaw.

Make the garlic mayo by stirring together 1/2 cup mayonnaise, 1 tablespoon pickle brine, 1 teaspoon Louisiana-style cayenne hot sauce, 1 teaspoon Dijon mustard, 1/2 teaspoon honey, 1/4 teaspoon smoked paprika, 1/4 teaspoon garlic powder, and 1/8 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt.
Refrigerate the garlic mayo until assembly.

Make the lime slaw by whisking together 3 tablespoons mayonnaise, 1 tablespoon lime juice, 1 tablespoon apple cider vinegar, 1 tablespoon pickle brine, 1 teaspoon honey, 1/4 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt, and 1/4 teaspoon black pepper in a medium bowl.
Add 3 cups plain shredded cabbage and carrot coleslaw mix and the 2 thinly sliced green onions to the lime dressing.
Toss the slaw for 30 seconds, until all of the cabbage is lightly coated, then refrigerate it until assembly.

Make the dry coating in a wide shallow bowl by whisking together 3/4 cup all-purpose flour, 1 cup panko breadcrumbs, 1/4 cup cornstarch, 1 teaspoon baking powder, 1 teaspoon smoked paprika, 1 teaspoon garlic powder, 1 teaspoon onion powder, 1/2 teaspoon cayenne pepper, 1 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt, 1/2 teaspoon black pepper, and 1/2 teaspoon MSG if using.
Drizzle 2 tablespoons of the buttermilk mixture into the dry coating and rub it through with your fingers until small shaggy clumps form.
Remove 1 chicken cutlet from the buttermilk mixture and let the excess drip back into the bowl for 3 to 5 seconds.
Press the chicken cutlet firmly into the dry coating on both sides.
Dip the coated chicken cutlet back into the buttermilk mixture for 1 second, then let the excess drip off.
Press the chicken cutlet into the dry coating a second time, packing the panko-flour mixture onto both sides until there are no wet spots.
Place the breaded chicken cutlet on the raw-chicken wire rack and repeat with the remaining 3 cutlets.
Let the breaded chicken rest on the raw-chicken wire rack for 10 minutes so the coating absorbs moisture and sticks.
While the chicken rests, spread 2 tablespoons softened unsalted butter over the cut sides of the 4 potato buns.
Heat the cast-iron skillet or heavy saute pan over medium heat, then toast the buttered buns cut-side down for 1 to 2 minutes, until golden brown.
Move the toasted buns to a plate and wipe out any loose crumbs from the skillet.
Pour 1 1/2 cups avocado oil into the skillet, or enough to make a 1/4-inch layer.
Heat the oil over medium to medium-high heat until it reaches 350 degrees F.
Fry 2 chicken cutlets at a time so the pan is not crowded.
Cook the first side for 3 to 4 minutes, until deep golden brown and crisp.
Flip the chicken cutlets with tongs and cook the second side for 2 to 3 minutes, until deep golden brown and the center of each cutlet reaches 165 degrees F.
Transfer the fried chicken cutlets to the clean wire rack, keeping them in a single layer so steam can escape.
Let the oil return to 350 degrees F before frying the remaining 2 chicken cutlets.

While the second batch fries, make the hot honey butter by warming 2 tablespoons unsalted butter, 1/4 cup honey, 2 teaspoons Louisiana-style cayenne hot sauce, 1 teaspoon apple cider vinegar, 1/4 teaspoon cayenne pepper, and 1/8 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt in a small saucepan or microwave-safe bowl until the butter melts.
Stir the hot honey butter until glossy and smooth.
Brush or spoon the hot honey butter over both sides of the fried chicken cutlets while they are still hot.
Spread about 1 tablespoon garlic mayo on each toasted bottom bun.
Add 1/4 cup dill pickle chips to each bottom bun.
Place 1 hot honey chicken cutlet on each bottom bun.
Top each chicken cutlet with about 1/2 cup lime slaw.
Spread about 1 tablespoon garlic mayo on each toasted top bun.
Close the sandwiches and serve immediately while the chicken crust is crisp.
notes: Use a thin Louisiana-style cayenne hot sauce. It should taste salty, vinegary, and peppery, not smoky or sweet.
Buy dill pickle chips, not sweet bread-and-butter pickles, because the brine seasons the chicken dip, garlic mayo, and slaw.
Pounding the chicken to 1/2 inch matters because the coating browns quickly; thick chicken can overcook on the outside before the center reaches 165 degrees F.
The 10-minute coating rest helps the dry flour patches hydrate so the crust clings in the skillet.
Use separate racks for raw breaded chicken and fried chicken. The clean rack also keeps the bottom crust from steaming soft.
Keep the oil between 325 degrees F and 350 degrees F while frying. If the coating browns before the chicken reaches 165 degrees F, lower the heat slightly.
The slaw should stay lightly dressed, not wet. If the coleslaw mix releases liquid while waiting, lift the slaw with tongs and leave extra dressing behind during assembly.
For a less spicy sandwich, reduce the cayenne pepper in the dry coating to 1/4 teaspoon and skip the 1/4 teaspoon cayenne pepper in the hot honey butter.
For a hotter sandwich, add 1/4 teaspoon extra cayenne pepper to the hot honey butter.
Serve right after brushing with hot honey butter so the crust stays crisp and the buns stay toasted.
title: Hot Honey Chicken Cutlet Sandwiches
